image_source.selection_reason: Skill requires openEuler 24.03 LTS as default verification image; repo CI uses openEuler 22.03 SP2

architecture.source: README.md - Hardware support section supports both X86 and ARM

recommended_image.value: openEuler 22.03 SP2 (from CI pipeline status) / pytorch/manylinux-builder:cpu-2.1 (from Dockerfile)

dependencies.source: README.md - From Source section; CMakeLists.txt line 1; ci/build.sh; ci/docker/X86/Dockerfile

ut_commands.value: cd test && python test_<name>.py OR python ci/access_control_test.py --all

sample_commands.value: bash ci/libtorch_resnet.sh (for resnet example); requires NPU hardware

value.name: CANN (Ascend AI Processor Software Stack)
value.install_method: Refer to CANN Installation guide at https://www.hiascend.com/cann
value.requirement: Required for runtime/execution, not for compilation. README says: 'complete the installation of CANN according to Ascend Auxiliary Software. To obtain the CANN installation package, refer to the CANN Installation'

value.name: hccl (Huawei Collective Communication Library)
value.install_method: Part of CANN installation - needed at runtime (libhccl.so)

special_dependencies.source: README.md - Installation section; CANN version table

documentation_notes: Build instructions reference Docker image approach (pytorch/manylinux-builder) rather than bare-metal installation
gcc version recommendation differs between ARM (10.2) and X86 (9.3.1); actual build succeeded with gcc 12.3.1
CANN download requires visiting hiascend.com - no direct wget link provided in README
No explicit test/sample commands documented in main README - found in test/README.md and ci/ directory

execution_log.command: dnf install -y gcc gcc-c++ cmake make (with Tsinghua mirrors)
execution_log.error: Initial attempt failed due to default repo timeout; resolved by configuring Tsinghua mirrors via sed on /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo

execution_log.command: dnf install -y python3-devel python3-pip python3-setuptools python3-wheel
pip3 install pyyaml setuptools wheel numpy (Tsinghua mirror)
git submodule init && git submodule update --depth 1
execution_log.output: Initialized 10 submodules (Tensorpipe, ge, graph-autofusion, runtime, fmt, googletest, nlohmann, op-plugin, torchair)
Build failed: OOM - cc1plus killed (used -j 20 all cores)
execution_log.error: c++: fatal error: Killed signal terminated program cc1plus

execution_log.output: Build succeeded. Wheel: torch_npu-2.12.0+gitunknown-cp311-cp311-linux_x86_64.whl (37.8MB). Build artifacts: libtorch_npu.so (116MB), libtensorpipe.so (3.6MB), libop_plugin_atb.so (1.2MB), libnpu_profiler.so (141KB)
ImportError: libhccl.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
execution_log.error: CANN runtime not installed - libhccl.so and other NPU driver libraries are required for runtime execution. These are part of the CANN (Ascend) software stack that only runs on NPU hardware.

execution_log.output: Skipped - cannot run without CANN/NPU hardware
execution_log.error: All tests require Ascend NPU hardware with CANN runtime installed. Tests cannot execute in a standard x86_64 container without NPU devices.

process_timeline.action: Read README.md, README.zh.md, CMakeLists.txt, ci/build.sh, ci/docker/X86/Dockerfile, setup.py, pyproject.toml, CONTRIBUTING.md
Pulled and started openEuler 24.03 LTS container
Installed gcc 12.3.1, cmake 3.27.9, make 4.4.1, python3-devel, pip packages (PyTorch 2.12.0+cpu, pyyaml, numpy, setuptools)
Build attempt with -j 20 (all cores) - OOM killed
Tested import torch_npu - failed due to missing CANN runtime (libhccl.so)
Cannot run unit tests or samples without NPU hardware and CANN runtime

ut.skip_reason: Unit tests require Ascend NPU hardware with CANN runtime installed. Tests cannot execute in a standard x86_64 container without NPU devices. Test commands documented at test/README.md: python test_<name>.py or python ci/access_control_test.py --all

sample.skip_reason: Examples require Ascend NPU hardware with CANN runtime. Examples include: libtorch_resnet, libtorch_npu (inference), libtorch_hccl (allreduce), libtorch_triton_ascend. All require NPU devices and CANN libraries for execution.

documentation_gaps: CANN download link is a general page URL (https://www.hiascend.com/cann) rather than direct package download links
No explicit command-line instructions for bare-metal build - README recommends Docker-based build
gcc version recommendation (9.3.1 for x86) differs from what's installed (12.3.1) - build succeeded anyway
Test commands not in main README - found in test/README.md and ci/ directory
No minimal test for verifying build success without NPU hardware
version.txt (2.13.0) does not match prebuilt PyTorch availability (2.12.0 was latest available from download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu)

problems_encountered.source: Standard Chinese mirror configuration for openEuler

problems_encountered.problem: OOM: cc1plus killed during compilation with -j 20
problems_encountered.solution: Set MAX_JOBS=8 environment variable (40% of 20 cores) to limit parallelism and reduce memory pressure; successful compile with this setting
problems_encountered.source: Skill requirement: max concurrent jobs <= 60% of cores; observed OOM at 100% utilization

problems_encountered.solution: Used --disable_torchair build flag; torchair requires CANN SDK and is optional for the build
Ran git submodule update --init --recursive to initialize TensorPipe's submodules (libuv, googletest, pybind11, libnop, secure_c) and torchair's submodules

problems_encountered.problem: Cannot execute tests/samples without NPU hardware
problems_encountered.solution: This is expected behavior - torch_npu is designed for Ascend NPU devices. Build verification is sufficient in this environment. Full testing requires: 1) NPU hardware (Atlas 800, 900, etc.), 2) CANN software stack installed, 3) NPU drivers loaded.
problems_encountered.source: README.md - Hardware support section lists supported devices; CANN installation at https://www.hiascend.com/cann

problems_encountered.solution: Minor cosmetic issue - version.txt correctly set to 2.12.0 but git describe may fail in shallow clone; doesn't affect functionality
